desc:
Though apparently used infrequently, this large tunnel must be visited at times
by some large heavy biped, judging by the faint footprints you can see in the
gravel and dust on the floor at your feet. The passageway travels from east to
west, where it narrows markedly. To the south a rough-hewn entry lets onto a
spacious room.
name: Large Tunnel

desc:
Filth surrounds you in this squalid cave, and the air hangs heavy with the vile
smell of putrefaction. Old bones and assorted junk litter the floor, and a pair
of enormous cots stand against one wall. Against the opposite wall is an
assembly of shelves and cabinets, too fine to be of trollish manufacture. But
despite the signs of heavy wear on all of this furniture, now only a few large,
ugly, wooden jugs and cups sit here.
name: Guard Cave

desc:
The floor beneath your feet is blanketed in a thick coating of pale grey dust,
marking the passage of time since this thoroughfare was last used. From the fine
workmanship, it is obvious that this tunnel was cut by Durin's folk in the
ancient days, and it has probably not been used since they fled Khazad-dum. To
the east, the passage opens out into a wider tunnel, whilst the way west is a
slightly more confined passage.
name: Disused Passageway

desc:
The musty air here feels thick with dust, but the passage in which you stand
seems sound and well-constructed. The walls, ceiling and floor are smooth and
featureless, and the tunnel cuts a bold east west path through the surrounding
bedrock.
name: Disused Passageway

desc:
Thick grey dust coats the floor of this tunnel. This thoroughfare has long been
disused, but the old, fine workmanship of the ancient dwarves remains apparent
in the smooth walls and squared dimensions. The passage continues east, but
dead-ends to the west. However, a small opening in the southern wall leads to a
natural rock grotto, beyond which can be seen a larger natural cavern. There
seems to be a large, still body of water filling the cavern, and the surface of
the water can just be seen from here.
name: Disused Passageway

desc:
This small granite enclave lies to the north of a dismal lake, which stretches
out across a mighty cavern, well beyond the reach of your feeble light. At the
back of the enclave a barren tunnel opens to the north, before bending to the
east, out of view. On the lake, twists of fog writhe above the surface of the
water, while the sombre silence is disturbed only by an occasional faint gurgle,
coming from somewhere in the distance.
name: Enclave at Lake's Edge

desc:
The gloomy waters of this cheerless lake have an unwholesome look about them.
The air here is chilled and moistened by small wisps of fog, which rise from the
surface of the stagnant water. The lake opens out in the vast natural cavern to
the south and west, but ends abruptly where the cave wall comes down to meet the
water to the east. To the north is a wide enclave in the rock, just above the
water-line. To the rear of this chamber a rough tunnel opening can barely be
glimpsed.
name: Shallow Lake

desc:
Cool, clammy air surrounds you, making your clothes cling sodden and limp to
your body, chilling you. Your light cannot penetrate far into the gloom, but you
can see that the lake stretches beyond you to the north and west. The wall to
the east of you is cold, smooth and beaded with moisture, and to the south, the
lake is bordered by a sandy shoreline.
name: Shallow Lake

desc:
To the north of this small sandy shoreline lies a vast dark underground lake.
The air here is cool and moist, and beads of moisture festoon the cave walls to
the south and west of you. To the east, a few feet above the shoreline, a large
tunnel can be seen, and a small runnel of water trickles from the lip of the
opening. All is still, but from the tunnel you can hear the dull echoes of heavy
footfalls.
name: By the Shore of a Dark Lake

desc:
The walls and ceiling of this passage are covered in moisture, which collects in
small beads, and drips to the floor below. At your feet, small rivulets of water
flow slowly to the west, where they trickle over the lip of an opening in the
cave wall. The air here is moist and still, except for a faint warm breeze,
which cuts across the tunnel from north to south.
name: Damp Passage

desc:
The walls here are mossy and damp, stretching far above your head as the stone
stairwell corkscrews up away from you. Though the spiral is quite tight, the
steps are broad and shallow, making this a safe and easy path to follow. A
gentle breeze wafts past you as you stand here, whispering strange tales of what
lies beyond you, to the north.
name: Damp Winding Stair

desc:
Water drips from the ceiling all around you, falling into small puddles on the
ground. Tiny rivulets of water channel down cracks in the mossy walls, and the
air about you smells damp and stale. The passageway runs generally from east to
west.
name: Damp Passage

desc:
Small puddles of water have collected on the gravelly floor, and are added to by
the drip drip drip from the ceiling above your head. To the east this damp
passage widens into the entrance to a massive cavern, and a reddish glow reaches
you from there, casting strange shadows which seem to dance a macabre jig about
you.
name: Damp Passage

desc:
There is a gentle, almost imperceptible slope here, with the northern end of
this tunnel lying only a little lower than the south. However the stone floor is
quite smooth, and has been made slick by moisture from the passageway to the
south, so maintaining a footing is not easy.
name: Dark Sloping Tunnel

desc:
It is hard to see the ground in front of you, but you can vaguely discern that
the tunnel here ascends to the south. Since it is quite smooth, it is at times a
little difficult to maintain a steady footing, and the featureless walls to
either side of you offer no hand-holds to assist you.
name: Dark Sloping Tunnel

desc:
At the north-western border of this large underground lake the darkness seems
quite impenetrable. Your faint light barely illuminates the whispy tendrils of
fog, which curl up above the dark, still surface of the water around you. All is
silent save for an occasional plop or gurgle, emanating from somewhere far out
in the lake beyond you. To the north and west the rock walls of this cavern loom
above you, offering no exit. You must instead cross the gloomy waters of the
lake to the south or east.
name: Dark Lake

desc:
You are on the dark still surface of a large underground lake. Tufts of fog curl
about the surface in the beam of your light, while every now and again a faint
plop or gurgle can be heard somewhere out in the waters; otherwise all remains
quiet. You can continue across the lake to the north and east; to the west is a
small pebbly beach, beyond which can be seen a passage of some kind.
name: Shallow Lake

desc:
Here a long pebbly beach defines the western end of a vast underground lake.
Ethereal wisps of moisture curl up from the dark, still surface of the water,
which lies undisturbed, save for a few tiny ripples, accompanied by a gurgle or
plopping sound, heard far out in the lake; otherwise all is quiet in this huge
natural cavern. Behind you a passage ascends rapidly to the west, and from that
direction a faint cool breeze is blowing.
name: Subterranean Lakeshore

desc:
A chilling current of air wafts past you from above, where this passage climbs
away to the north. The gravelly floor falls away to the east, where the tunnel
opens out into a vast natural cavern. In the distance you hear the faint sound
of dripping water.
name: Steep Passage

desc:
The floor in this small passage descends rapidly southward, and rises sharply to
the west of you. Though steep, the ground is fairly rough and dry, making it
quite easy to keep your footing. A cold breeze blows down from the passage
above.
name: Steep Passage

desc:
Descending rapidly from west to east, the floor of this steep passage is fairly
rough, but a little moist, making it difficult to avoid slipping and falling. A
faint but frigid draught assails you from the passage to the west.
name: Steep Passage

desc:
This small room glistens white with ice wherever you look. A profusion of
icicles hang from the ceiling, and treacherous sheets of ice carpet the floor.
Small icy curtains conceal the walls, resembling petrified waterfalls. A faint,
yet freezing, airflow is coming from somewhere in the northern wall, but the
only obvious exit is to the east, down a steep passage.
name: Ice Cave

desc:
The floor and walls of this narrow corridor are smooth and slick with ice. A
frigid wind blows steadily from the west, dancing between the forest of spear-
like icicles which cover the ceiling, and seeming to penetrate through your
garments. A faint hissing noise can be heard from somewhere in the south wall of
this passage, which seems to end abruptly here.
name: An Icy Passage

desc:
A freezing wind whips into this chamber from the north, and whistles past you
eastward into a narrow corridor. This freezing-cold cavern is bare and quite
featureless, just a small room neatly chiselled from the surrounding granite. A
small patch of sky can barely be seen through the exit to the north, which must
lead outside the mountain, and just beyond the opening you can see a narrow
ledge, where one might perhaps stand.
name: A Freezing Cave

desc:
This narrow ledge has neither parapet nor rail, typical of dwarven design. A
fierce blast of icy wind rolls past you, down the mountainside, as you gaze out
at the immense spectacle of the valley of Hollin, which lies several thousand
feet below, stretching away into a misty haze. To either side, and above you,
are the sheer sides of the Misty Mountains, too steep to be scaled here. The
only possible exit from this place is to turn into the forbidding darkness of
Moria, to the south.
name: On a High Ledge

desc:
The stairway stretches beyond you in both directions. To the east it begins a
tight downward spiral, whilst to the west it follows a less winding path. Each
tread of the stair has been carved with infinite care, and shows little sign of
wear. However, here and there a small crack or chip can be seen, as if some
noisome beast has attempted to destroy the fine workmanship.
name: The Long Stair

desc:
As straight as an arrow, the broad staircase climbs away from you to the north.
To the east it descends, tracing a more twisted path, and narrowing markedly.
The ascent is not a great one, with each individual step being quite shallow,
making this an easy way for dwarf and giant alike.
name: The Long Stair

desc:
To north and south, the stairway seems to stretch endlessly, its undeviating
path becoming monotonous to follow. Each broad step of the stair seems like the
last, crafted to an exacting standard; this could only be the work of Durin's
folk.
name: The Long Stair

desc:
A long, straight stairway stretches before you to the south. Though there is no
handrail, the way looks quite safe. Each broad, shallow step has been carved
precisely, so that all are identical, and the smooth surface of the stone is
quite dry. No crack or chip can be seen, which might cause an unwary traveller
to lose his footing.
name: The Long Stair

desc:
Sloping downward from east to west, this tunnel follows a sinuous path, which
seems to twist and writhe through the solid rock. Small glistening beads of
water draw your eyes to the wall, as they slowly from into tiny streams which
run down to the floor and away from you into a small hollow west of here. To the
south, an unadorned archway leads to a long stairwell.
name: Dark Snaking Passage

desc:
In an abrupt turn, the tunnel swings north and east, climbing away from you in
both directions. Water has collected in this small hollow, but seems to be
seeping slowly through narrow cracks in the floor. Though it looks fairly deep,
it appears to be possible to wade through it, to reach the tunnel beyond.
name: Dark Snaking Passage

desc:
Treading such a convoluted path is becoming quite dizzying. As the tunnel weaves
its way through the cold, dark rock, it also climbs and falls, as if unable to
find the right course. Only a sharp eye would notice the quartz intrusion, which
traces a delicate vein through the granite walls. Perhaps it was this which the
dwarven miners were following, in search of deposits of emeralds, pink
morganite, and aquamarine.
name: Dark Snaking Passage

desc:
The tortuous route of this tunnel now takes you north or west. Small flecks of
bright shiny quartz twinkle out from the smooth rock walls, but clearly this
passageway offered no promise of a great wealth of minerals or ore, and so the
dwarven miners pressed on in their search, so many years ago.
name: Dark Snaking Passage

desc:
Though the tunnel is still quite flexuous, here the turns are shorter, and
frequently switch direction, so that it is possible to see for quite a long
distance, both north and south, despite the twists and turns. Here too, the
floor begins to undulate gently, rising and falling every fifty or sixty feet
along the tunnel.
name: Dark Snaking Passage

desc:
The tunnel sweeps south and east from here, following a seemingly aimless course
in its progress through the mountain. But though its path seems a random one,
this passage is nevertheless well-wrought, with neatly squared walls, and a
fine, level floor.
name: Dark Snaking Passage

desc:
A simple squared opening to the north marks the end of a long snaking tunnel,
which meanders off to the west of here, and disappears from view. The hand which
chiselled this place was a skilled one, for the walls are well-crafted, but this
passage was never intended to a part of the living chambers of the dwarves;
rather, it seems to be an exploratory tunnel, hewn from the rock in search of
new ore.
name: Dark Snaking Passage

desc:
Carved with inexorable patience and care, this chamber still retains the same
inherent strength its builders intended, before their flight from Moria. But its
nature has twisted, with a more malevolent and constricting atmosphere, which
steals the courage from your soul.
name: Chamber

desc:
As the floor dips and rises with each step you take, it is rather difficult to
proceed here. Trying to steady yourself by reaching out to the wall would be a
big mistake though, as its surface is quite rough and unfinished, with large
outcroppings of rock, which tear at your clothes as you pass.
name: Rough-hewn Tunnel

desc:
Rubble and debris are scattered about the floor, as if this tunnel had only
recently been chipped out of the rock. Somehow, the ceiling above you seems less
solid than in the other tunnels, and a small dustfall occurs even as you stand
here.
name: Rough-hewn Tunnel

desc:
A jagged hole in the rockface to the east opens out into a large cavern. To the
west, this rugged passageway continues, its floor undulating in a hap- hazard
fashion. The roof above your head rises and falls to a rhythm of its own, making
this tunnel at times soaring, at times cramped and claustrophobic.
name: Rough-hewn Tunnel

desc:
Dried blood and excrement mingle with the dirt and gravel on the floor of this
large natural cavern. Massive stone benches have been set around the walls to
all sides, and at the north-eastern corner of the cavern stand a few stout
pikes, atop each of which rests a severed head. This cave must serve as a
meeting place and arena for the trolls, and it can only be imagined how many
poor beasts have perished here, or how many weary travellers.
name: Large Ominous Cavern

desc:
The stench here is appalling. This wide, sloping tunnel has been forced into
service as a storeroom for a hoard of rotting corpses and detached body parts.
Blood oozes from one still warm body, and seeps through the gravel, tracing a
downward path to the north. Another cadaver seems alive with a quivering mass of
maggots.
name: Smelly Tunnel

desc:
The roadway is jagged and pebbled, making it a treacherous path to follow. Deep
depressions in the rocky walls enhance the shadowy feeling of this place, and
send echoes, which bounce and reverberate around you, as if playfully taunting
you. A tiny trickle of water creeps southward across the sloping floor.
name: A Dark, Wide Road

desc:
Large, shattered blocks of masonry lie discarded to either side of the road, and
smaller pieces of debris clutter the entire area, though the origin of this
material is far from obvious. The floor, walls and ceiling of this passage are
so smooth and straight, that it is clear they were carved by the dwarves of
ancient Khazad-dum, whose pride in their work would never have allowed them to
leave such a fine roadway strewn with rubble.
name: A Dark Road

desc:
No sound intrudes to break the stillness here, save for the repeating echo of
your own footfalls. In the impenetrable gloom of Moria, this cold, dark roadway
makes its way onward, descending gently, but perceptibly, to the north.
name: A Wide Roadway

desc:
Four columns, cunningly crafted to resemble large oak trees, support the roof of
this magnificent chamber. The walls are decorated with a delicate design, made
up of tiny tiles of pale, rose quartz, dark green malachite, and highly
polished, black obsidian. Here, the wide, dark roadway, from Azaghal's Way,
above, intersects with the more opulent Avenue of Gold.
name: A Junction Chamber

desc:
Smooth, straight walls, a level, flat floor, and an arched roof mark this broad
passageway as the work of the dwarves, and not of Moria's current barbaric
inhabitants. The granite walls are perfectly featureless, save for a delicate
inlay of gold, of such craftsmanship that it defied the plundering hoards of
orcs and trolls, remaining intact through the long years of their occupation of
these halls and caverns.
name: Avenue of Gold

desc:
Two delicate parallel lines of inlaid gold run the length of each wall of this
wide passage, but the grey granite is otherwise featureless, and is perfectly
smooth. A small arch in the northern wall, undecorated, as if to avoid any
attention, opens into a modest chamber, whilst to the east, a larger hall can be
seen.
name: Avenue of Gold

desc:
The Avenue of Gold comes to an end here, in this gracefully arched chamber,
which was perhaps built to offer a resting place for those travellers wearied by
their journey along Azaghal's Way, above. The arches and pillars divide the room
into a number of smaller cubicles, which would have afforded some degree of
silence and solitude.
name: An Arched Hall

desc:
This small chamber perhaps once served as a resting hall for weary dwarven
miners. Small niches, cut into the west wall, hold what appear to be the
shattered and rotten remains of tables and chairs, whilst at the centre of the
room, stands what can only have been a stone well. Now, however, its walls have
been torn asunder, large blocks of stone obstruct the wide, round opening, and
instead of fresh water, only dust and rubble can be seen below. Where once
Durin's folk relaxed after their labours, now a tribe of orcs has set up a guard
post.
name: A Domed Chamber

desc:
No vast hoard of gems lies here, but the walls still twinkle with tiny flecks of
blue aquamarine, and bright green emerald. Too dispersed among the granite to be
worth mining, these tiny deposits have instead been polished within their dull,
grey setting, to produce a passageway of startling beauty.
name: The Emerald Way

desc:
The slightest sound comes rumbling back from the caverns to the west, in a
cacophony of repeating echoes. The riches of those caves are scarcely hinted at
by the delicate sprinkling of beryl in the walls here, yet still they sparkle
and glitter, with a promise of what lies beyond.
name: The Emerald Way

desc:
Networks of stone walkways criss-cross this vast cavern, which rears high above
you, and reaches into the darkness below. The walls are uneven and ragged,
marked still by the scores and narrow gouged clefts, left there, so long ago, by
the dwarven miners.
name: The Mines of Khulakal

desc:
Narrow walkways track from pillar to slender pillar across the cavern, linking
the many workings of the mine. In ancient days, this must have been the site of
great activity, as the dwarves delved diligently into the rock, but today all is
still and silent, lending an air of forboding to the place.
name: The Mines of Khulakal

desc:
Jagged nooks and crannies punctuate the rockface of this massive cavern. Many
stretch deep enough into the walls to be small tunnels, but it appears that most
dead-end after only a short distance, for these are no passageways, but the
ancient diggings of this huge mine complex.
name: The Mines of Khulakal

desc:
Perhaps this was originally just another small tunnel, tracing a precious vein
of beryl deep into the rockface, but at some time, it was enlarged into a small
chamber. Dwarven runes, carved into the south wall, above a small, arched exit
suggest that this place was hewn by Durin's people, but the function of this
chamber is not readily apparent.
name: A Small Chamber

desc:
Rough stairs, and narrow walkways once connected the various levels of workings
in this mine, but most of these now lie in disrepair, long since rendered
impassable by both the ravages of time, and the wanton destruction of the orcs
and trolls, who have made Moria their home. Now only one narrow pathway spans
the levels here, twisting and turning, with numerous tight switch-backs, into
the darkness below.
name: The Mines of Khulakal

desc:
This narrow passageway links the small room north of here to a larger chamber,
which can be seen beyond a sturdy iron portcullis, to the south. Judging by the
smooth, straight walls and the low, level ceiling above, this corridor was
crafted by the dwarves. And yet, strangely, small scores can be seen in the hard
granite floor. Perhaps they were left there by whoever assailed the grating to
the south, whose cunning manufacture has defied any attempts at opening it, and
whose strength has obviously withstood massive abuse.
name: A Narrow Passageway

desc:
The walls of this passageway sparkle with mystery, hinting at a history long-
forgotten by all but the dwarves, who once delved deep in Khazad-dum. Only on
true riches did they expend their labour, and so this place still glitters with
tiny deposits of emerald, embedded firmly in the dull, grey granite.
name: The Emerald Way

desc:
The broad passageway sweeps to the south here, in a graceful curve. No jagged
edges mar the walls and floor, and the ceiling, above, is smooth and level.
Every detail of this thoroughfare lends credence to the tales, so often told, of
the vast skill of the dwarves in the working of stone.
name: The Emerald Way

desc:
Never does the warmth of the sun reach this cold, stone avenue, nor does the
pale light of the moon ever soften the darkness. Here, hapless surface- dwellers
stumble, disoriented by the endless gloom. And yet, even this place has a
certain beauty: embedded throughout the dull, grey granite of the walls are
innumerable small deposits of sparkling green beryl.
name: The Emerald Way

desc:
At the sound of approaching footfalls, dozens of small creatures scuttle away,
unseen, retreating into the many clefts and fissures in the walls of this
passageway. The floor here is cracked and pitted, with numerous small rocks
scattered about, and even the ceiling seems somewhat precarious.
name: The Emerald Way

desc:
This tiny passageway is in stark contrast with the grand hallway to the south.
Its ceiling, though neatly arched, hangs low, compared to the lofty Emerald Way,
and its walls stand only one short pace apart from each-other. Clearly, this
route was intended to be one of the dwarves' more private accessways, in the
ancient days of Khazad-dum.
name: A Winding Accessway

desc:
No mark of hammer, chisel or pick is left to mar the flawless surfaces of the
walls here, and the arched roof curves smoothly above. Even the floor bears no
blemish. It would almost seem that this tunnel were the work of Aule himself, so
skilfully was it wrought by his people.
name: A Winding Accessway

desc:
A vaulted ceiling hangs low in this small, meandering passageway, and the cold,
dark, granite walls stand close together, creating rather a claustrophobic
feeling, which is enhanced by the stagnancy of the air, and the impenetrable
gloom.
name: A Winding Accessway

desc:
No rough-hewn chamber, this cramped little cavern bears the hallmarks of the
craftsmen of Durin's folk. The walls, floor and ceiling are perfectly smooth,
and the dimensions of the room regular. However, massive cracks have scarred the
west wall, and it must have been through these that the icy cold water has
poured, flooding the entire room.
name: A Water-filled Chamber

desc:
Just as it was further to the north, the south end of the chamber is well-
wrought, with walls, floor and ceiling all polished to a smooth finish. It would
appear that this small passageway was constructed in the days when the Mines of
Khulakal, above, were active with hoardes of busy dwarven delvers. Set at the
very centre of the roof above you is a simple, yet skilfully crafted stone
trapdoor.
name: A Water-filled Chamber

desc:
Dust blankets everything within the room, billowing up in choking clouds at
every slight movement. The air hangs heavy and still, as if time itself were
suspended in this cramped chamber. The walls and floor are smooth, the cold,
hard granite polished by the skilled hands of dwarven artisans, so many ages
before.
name: Khulakal's Treasury

desc:
Though of quite a fair size, this narrow corridor would probably be considered
small and secretive for trolls. It is poorly crafted, obviously not the work of
Durin's folk, and ends to the east in a crude rock door. At the west end, the
northern wall seems cracked and uneven, and weak beams of light pierce through
these chinks from a chamber beyond.
name: Secret Passage

desc:
An array of rather grisly trophies hang from the walls of this large chamber,
which appears to be lavishly decorated, although in a manner which could only
appeal to a troll. Well-crafted, yet rather ugly, furnishings fill this room: a
huge oak table, bearing silver utensils, stands at the centre of the room, a
massive oak dresser and a number of smaller oak cabinets rest in recesses in the
walls, and a colossal stone bed dominates the north-east corner of the room.
name: The King's Cave

desc:
A rather small and crudely carved cave, this is the secret treasury of the king
of this small band of trolls. Several strong-boxes line a shelf, and below them
on the ground are a couple of large wooden chests. Along the far wall are a
number of shields, several spears and a huge stone club. Beside these stand
three sealed barrels, perhaps containing treasure of some other kind.
name: The King's Private Hoard

desc:
Black-rocked and cold, with small, dark niches hewn crudely along each rough
stone wall, this cavern has clearly been chosen as a resting place for the
trolls. The gravelly floor is strewn with all manner of litter, and through a
jagged opening to the north, a strong smell wafts into the room.
name: Sleeping Cave

desc:
A huge pit, reeking with rotting matter of all types, lies just to the north.
Deep and dark, smooth-sided and sheer, anything dropped here will probably be
gone for good. The air is heavy with with decay and filled with the high-
pitched chatter of rodents.
name: Garbage Pit
